---
title: "Day 1: Points"
---


```{r}     

# Day 1: Points - Map of All US Schools by Level (Elementary, Middle, High)
# Using R and the EDSurvey package to create a map of all US schools, categorized by level.

# Load required libraries
library(EdSurvey)
library(tidyverse)
library(sf)
library(tmap)

# Load or acquire school location data (replace this with your actual data source)
# Example: Assuming we are using CCD (Common Core of Data) data available in EDSurvey
ccd_data <- readNAEP(system.file("extdata/data/ccd_sch_1718.sav", package = "EdSurvey"))

# Data preparation
# Filter for required columns and clean data for mapping
schools_df <- ccd_data %>%
  select(schoolID, schoolName, latitude, longitude, schoolLevel) %>%
  filter(!is.na(latitude) & !is.na(longitude)) %>%
  mutate(level_category = case_when(
    grepl("Elementary", schoolLevel, ignore.case = TRUE) ~ "Elementary",
    grepl("Middle", schoolLevel, ignore.case = TRUE) ~ "Middle",
    grepl("High", schoolLevel, ignore.case = TRUE) ~ "High",
    TRUE ~ "Other"
  ))

# Convert to spatial object
schools_sf <- st_as_sf(schools_df, coords = c("longitude", "latitude"), crs = 4326)

# Create the map using tmap
tm <- tm_shape(schools_sf) +
  tm_dots(col = "level_category",
          palette = c("#1f78b4", "#33a02c", "#e31a1c"),
          size = 0.05,
          title = "School Level") +
  tm_layout(title = "Map of All US Schools by Level",
            legend.outside = TRUE)

# Save the map to file or display it
tmap_save(tm, "day1_school_map.png")
print(tm)


```
